title: CSS Box Model Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential rules for defining the size and spacing of every element on a web page using the CSS Box Model.

questions.question: What is the primary role of the 'CSS Box Model' in a web application?
options: To define the website's visual styling and layout (colors, fonts, etc.).
To define the size and spacing for every element on a page (content, padding, border, and margin).
To provide a database that is only accessible during a specific time on the server.
Both 'To define the website's visual styling' and 'To provide a database' are correct.

questions.question: Which of these is the MOST recommended box model property to make layout calculation easier?
options: box-sizing: content-box;
box-sizing: border-box;
box-sizing: layout-box;
box-sizing: calc-box;

questions.question: In the CSS Box Model, what is the 'Margin'?
options: The clear area directly inside the border that separates the content from its edge.
The clear area outside the border that separates the element from its neighbors.
The border itself around the element.
The center area where the image or text lives.

questions.question: In the CSS Box Model, what is the 'Padding'?
options: The clear area directly inside the border that separates the content from its edge.
The clear area outside the border that separates the element from its neighbors.
The border itself around the element.
The center area where the image or text lives.

questions.question: By default, the 'width' property refers specifically to which area of the CSS Box Model?
options: Content + Padding
Content
Content + Padding + Border
Content + Margin
